package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type RouteStatusReport struct {
	Routes          []RouteStatusEntry
	GeneratedAt     time.Time
	GlobalCooldowns []CooldownState
	// RoutingQuality holds the three first-class routing-quality metrics
	// over a recent window (last RouteStatusRoutingQualityWindow requests).
	RoutingQuality RoutingQualityMetrics
}
    RouteStatusReport is returned by RouteStatus.

    RoutingQuality (ADR-006 §5) is the operator-facing measure of how often
    auto-routing produces an acceptable decision. It is intentionally
    distinct from per-(provider, model) provider-reliability surfaced on each
    RouteCandidateStatus: the two compose, and conflating them is the UI bug
    ADR-006 fixes.

package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type HarnessInfo struct {
	Name                 string
	Type                 string // "native" | "subprocess"
	Available            bool
	Path                 string
	Error                string
	IsLocal              bool
	IsSubscription       bool
	AutoRoutingEligible  bool
	TestOnly             bool
	ExactPinSupport      bool
	DefaultModel         string   // built-in default model when no override is supplied
	SupportedPermissions []string // subset of {"safe","supervised","unrestricted"}
	SupportedReasoning   []string // values such as {"low","medium","high","xhigh","max"}
	CostClass            string   // "local" | "cheap" | "medium" | "expensive"
	Quota                *QuotaState
	Account              *AccountStatus
	UsageWindows         []UsageWindow
	LastError            *StatusError
	CapabilityMatrix     HarnessCapabilityMatrix
}
    HarnessInfo describes a registered harness as defined in CONTRACT-003.

package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type QuotaState struct {
	Windows    []harnesses.QuotaWindow `json:"windows"`
	CapturedAt time.Time               `json:"captured_at"`
	Fresh      bool                    `json:"fresh"`
	Source     string                  `json:"source,omitempty"`
	Status     string                  `json:"status,omitempty"` // ok|stale|unavailable|unauthenticated|unknown
	LastError  *StatusError            `json:"last_error,omitempty"`
}
    QuotaState is a live quota snapshot for a harness. Nil means not applicable.

package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type ProviderInfo struct {
	Name           string
	Type           string // "openai" | "openrouter" | "lmstudio" | "omlx" | "ollama" | "anthropic" | "virtual"
	BaseURL        string
	Endpoints      []ServiceProviderEndpoint
	Status         string // "connected" | "unreachable" | "error: <msg>"
	ModelCount     int
	Capabilities   []string       // e.g. {"tool_use","streaming","json_mode"}
	IsDefault      bool           // matches the configured default_provider
	DefaultModel   string         // per-provider configured default model, if any
	CooldownState  *CooldownState // nil if not in cooldown
	Auth           AccountStatus
	EndpointStatus []EndpointStatus
	Quota          *QuotaState
	UsageWindows   []UsageWindow
	LastError      *StatusError
}
    ProviderInfo describes a provider with live status per CONTRACT-003.

package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type AccountStatus struct {
	Authenticated   bool
	Unauthenticated bool
	Email           string
	PlanType        string
	OrgName         string
	Source          string
	CapturedAt      time.Time
	Fresh           bool
	Detail          string
}
    AccountStatus describes authentication/account state without exposing
    provider-specific native files to consumers.

package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type UsageWindow struct {
	Name                string
	Source              string
	CapturedAt          time.Time
	Fresh               bool
	InputTokens         int
	OutputTokens        int
	TotalTokens         int
	CacheReadTokens     int
	CacheWriteTokens    int
	ReasoningTokens     int
	CostUSD             float64
	KnownCostUSD        *float64
	UnknownCostSessions int
}
    UsageWindow describes normalized usage attribution over a time window.
    Empty token/cost totals mean the service has no historical usage source yet.

package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type EndpointStatus struct {
	Name          string
	BaseURL       string
	ProbeURL      string
	Status        string // connected|unreachable|unauthenticated|error|unknown
	Source        string
	CapturedAt    time.Time
	Fresh         bool
	LastSuccessAt time.Time
	ModelCount    int
	LastError     *StatusError
}
    EndpointStatus describes one configured provider endpoint probe.

package fizeau // import "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"

type RouteCandidateStatus struct {
	Provider                string
	Model                   string
	Priority                int
	Healthy                 bool
	Cooldown                *CooldownState
	RecentLatencyMS         float64 // observation-derived; 0 when unavailable
	ProviderReliabilityRate float64 // 0-1; 0 when unavailable. Legacy success-rate field, relabeled per ADR-006 §5 to disambiguate from routing-quality.
}
    RouteCandidateStatus describes a single candidate within a route.

    ProviderReliabilityRate is the per-(provider, model) observed completion
    rate (the legacy "success rate" metric). Per ADR-006 §5 it is labeled
    "provider reliability" to distinguish it from RoutingQualityMetrics on the
    parent report — the two metrics measure different things and are surfaced as
    separate fields.

That means most of the DDx-local interpretation can be removed rather than papered over, and I’m checking the GraphQL read model to see which bits can be wired straight through versus which ones should remain explicitly unresolved.

The compile issue is fixed; the full server/cmd test sweep is still running. I’m letting it finish rather than polling aggressively so I can catch the first real regression if there is one.

# DDx Root Makefile
# Builds CLI and copies to root for easy access

# Build variables
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
CLI_DIR = cli
ROOT_BINARY = ddx

.PHONY: all build clean test lint install help cli-build cli-clean cli-test cli-lint cli-bead-schema cli-skill-schema bead-schema skill-schema docs-routing-lint

# Default target - build CLI and copy to root
all: build

# Build CLI and copy to root
build: cli-build
	@echo "Copying CLI binary to root..."
	cp $(CLI_DIR)/build/ddx $(ROOT_BINARY)
	@echo "✅ DDx CLI built and available as ./$(ROOT_BINARY)"

# Clean all build artifacts
clean: cli-clean
	@echo "Cleaning root binary..."
	rm -f $(ROOT_BINARY)

# Run tests
test: cli-test

# Validate the shared bead schema
bead-schema: cli-bead-schema

# Validate bundled skill metadata
skill-schema: cli-skill-schema

# Run linter
lint: cli-lint

# Check docs for unallowlisted legacy routing vocabulary
docs-routing-lint:
	@echo "Checking docs for legacy routing references..."
	bash scripts/check-legacy-routing-docs.sh

# Install locally
install: build
	@echo "Installing DDx locally..."
	cp $(ROOT_BINARY) $(HOME)/.local/bin/ddx
	@echo "✅ DDx installed to ~/.local/bin/ddx"

# Format code
fmt:
	@echo "Formatting Go code..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && go fmt ./...

# CLI-specific targets (delegate to cli/Makefile)
cli-build:
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) build

cli-clean:
	@echo "Cleaning CLI build artifacts..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) clean

cli-test:
	@echo "Running CLI tests..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) test

cli-bead-schema:
	@echo "Validating bead schema..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) bead-schema

cli-skill-schema:
	@echo "Validating skill metadata..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) skill-schema

cli-lint:
	@echo "Running CLI linter..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) lint

cli-deps:
	@echo "Installing CLI dependencies..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) deps

cli-update-deps:
	@echo "Updating CLI dependencies..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) update-deps

# Development targets
dev: build
	@echo "Running DDx in development mode..."
	./$(ROOT_BINARY) $(ARGS)

# Build for all platforms
build-all:
	@echo "Building for all platforms..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) build-all

# Create release
release:
	@echo "Creating release..."
	cd $(CLI_DIR) && $(MAKE) release

# MCP server management (uses local .claude/settings.local.json by default)
mcp-list: build
	@echo "Listing available MCP servers..."
	./$(ROOT_BINARY) mcp list

mcp-install: build
	@echo "Installing MCP server to local project configuration..."
	@if [ -z "$(SERVER)" ]; then \
		echo "❌ Error: SERVER variable required. Usage: make mcp-install SERVER=server-name"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi
	./$(ROOT_BINARY) mcp install $(SERVER) --config-path .claude/settings.local.json --yes

mcp-install-global: build
	@echo "Installing MCP server to global configuration..."
	@if [ -z "$(SERVER)" ]; then \
		echo "❌ Error: SERVER variable required. Usage: make mcp-install-global SERVER=server-name"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi
	./$(ROOT_BINARY) mcp install $(SERVER) --yes

# DDx CLI Makefile

# Build variables
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"

# Build directory
BUILD_DIR = build

# Platforms to build for
PLATFORMS = \
	darwin/amd64 \
	darwin/arm64 \
	linux/amd64 \
	linux/arm64 \
	windows/amd64

.PHONY: all build clean test lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build frontend-rebuild frontend-verify copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql

# Default target
all: clean deps test build

# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/index.html

frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)

$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
	@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
frontend-rebuild:
	@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
frontend-verify:
	@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
	@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
	missing=0; \
	for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
		path=".$$ref"; \
		if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
			echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
			missing=$$((missing+1)); \
		fi; \
	done; \
	if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
		echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  ok"

# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/

# Build for current platform
build: copy-skills frontend-build
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .

# Build for all platforms
build-all: clean deps copy-skills
	@echo "Building for all platforms..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	@echo "Building current platform..."
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
	@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
		OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
		ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
		echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
		GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
		if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
			mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
		fi; \
	done

# Install dependencies
deps:
	@echo "Installing dependencies..."
	go mod download
	go mod tidy

# Run tests
test: copy-skills
	@echo "Running tests..."
	go test -v ./...

# Run the GraphQL perf harness (ddx-9ce6842a). Seeds a 10 × 500 bead fixture
# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override
# the fixture size or the report date via BENCH_REPORT_DATE.
BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)
BENCH_REPORT_DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
bench-graphql:
	@echo "Running GraphQL perf harness (fixture: 10 × 500 beads = 5,000)..."
	@mkdir -p $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR=$(BENCH_REPORT_DIR) \
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DATE=$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE) \
	go test ./internal/server/perf/ -run TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline -v -timeout 5m
	@echo "Baseline report written to $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)/$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE)-baseline.md"

# Validate the shared bead record schema
bead-schema:
	@echo "Validating bead record schema..."
	go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestBeadRecordSchema' -count=1

# Validate bundled skill metadata
skill-schema:
	@echo "Validating bundled skill metadata..."
	go run . skills check ../skills internal/skills

# Run linter
lint:
	@echo "Running linter..."
	@if command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		golangci-lint run; \
	else \
		echo "golangci-lint not found, running go vet instead"; \
		go vet ./...; \

Implemented and committed as `6d69dc6` with message `refactor: consume harness dto fields directly [ddx-e543f054]`.

What changed:
- [cli/internal/server/providers.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/providers.go)
  - Removed the DDx-local `RoutingSignalSnapshot` shim and the old default-model/quota/auth/cost helpers.
  - Switched provider summary/detail assembly to read `HarnessInfo` fields directly.
  - Built `models`, `auth_state`, `quota_headroom`, `signal_sources`, `freshness_ts`, and `request_fit` from upstream DTO data.
- [cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go)
  - `HarnessStatuses` now maps directly from `HarnessInfo` via a new helper instead of the rate-limit cache bridge.
- [cli/internal/server/providers_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/providers_test.go)
  - Updated to assert direct DTO-driven values and removed the old synthetic signal-source enum check.
- [cli/internal/server/graphql/providers_unified_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-e543f054-20260505T080913-e4877f7c/cli/internal/server/graphql/providers_unified_test.go)
